changed from the original:         1. `UUPSUpgradeable` -> `HMACGuardedUUPS` in inheritance         2. `ReentrancyGuardUpgradeable` -> `ReentrancyGuard` (OZ v5.6 reorg)         3. `__UUPSUpgradeable_init()` removed from initializer (no-op in v5)         4. `__HMACGuardedUUPS_init(_initialCommitment)` added to initializer         5. Initializer takes additional parameter `bytes32 _initialCommitment`         6. `_authorizeUpgrade` override now combines AccessControl role check            (preserved from original) with HMACGuardedUUPS transient flag check         All business logic \u2014 deposits, withdrawals, emergency mode, oracle         management, treasury \u2014 is unchanged from Zoth's deployed contract.         Storage layout is identical (verified: all OZ v5.0 namespace strings         match v5.6 strings, so namespaced storage hashes are identical).","errors":{"AccessControlBadConfirmation()":[{"details":"The caller of a function is not the expected one. this."},"RoleGranted(bytes32,address,address)":{"details":"Emitted when `account` is granted `role`. `sender` is the account that originated the contract call. This account bears the admin role (for the granted role). Expected in cases where the role was granted using the internal {AccessControl-_grantRole}."},"RoleRevoked(bytes32,address,address)":{"details":"Emitted when `account` is revoked `role`. `sender` is the account that originated the contract call:   - if using `revokeRole`, it is the admin role bearer   - if using `renounceRole`, it is the role bearer (i.e. `account`)"},"RouterSet(address)":{"details":"Router can only be set once and is critical for deposit operations","params":{"router":"Address of the newly set router contract"}},"SecondaryAssetOperation(address,address,uint256,bool)":{"details":"Secondary asset operation tracking event","params":{"amount":"Amount involved","asset":"Secondary asset involved","isDeposit":"Whether operation was deposit","user":"User involved in operation"}},"StableWhitelistStatusChanged(address,bool)":{"params":{"status":"New whitelist status","user":"Address that was affected"}},"Unpaused(address)":{"details":"Emitted when the pause is lifted by `account`."},"Upgraded(address)":{"details":"Emitted when the implementation is upgraded."},"WithdrawProcessed(address,address,uint256)":{"details":"Withdrawal tracking event","params":{"amount":"Amount withdrawn","asset":"Asset withdrawn","user":"User who withdrew"}}},"kind":"dev","methods":{"WS(address)":{"params":{"_ws":"Address of the withdrawal system contract"}},"addAsset(address,string)":{"details":"This functionality is disabled in this version"},"constructor":{"custom:oz-upgrades-unsafe-allow":"constructor","details":"Constructor is disabled as this is an upgradeable contract"},"getEmergencyStatus()":{"details":"Used to check vault status before operations","returns":{"isEmergencyMode":"Whether emergency mode is active","isPaused":"Whether vault is paused","timeUntilNextAction":"Time until next emergency action allowed"}},"getPrimaryAsset()":{"details":"Primary asset cannot be changed after deployment","returns":{"_0":"address Address of primary asset"}},"getRoleAdmin(bytes32)":{"details":"Returns the admin role that controls `role`. See {grantRole} and {revokeRole}. To change a role's admin, use {_setRoleAdmin}."},"getSupportedAssets()":{"details":"Primary asset is always first in the array","returns":{"_0":"Array of supported asset addresses"}},"grantRole(bytes32,address)":{"details":"Grants `role` to `account`. If `account` had not been already granted `role`, emits a {RoleGranted} event. Requirements: - the caller must have ``role``'s admin role. May emit a {RoleGranted} event."},"handleDeposit(address,address,uint256)":{"details":"Only handles primary asset deposits, reverts for secondary assets","params":{"amount":"Amount to deposit","asset":"Address of asset being deposited"},"returns":{"_0":"success Whether deposit was successful"}},"handleWithdraw(address,address,uint256)":{"details":"Only handles primary asset withdrawals","params":{"amount":"Amount to withdraw","asset":"Address of asset being withdrawn","user":"Address of withdrawing user"},"returns":{"_0":"success Whether withdrawal was successful"}},"hasRole(bytes32,address)":{"details":"Returns `true` if `account` has been granted `role`."},"isAssetSupported(address)":{"params":{"asset":"Address of the asset to check"},"returns":{"_0":"Whether the asset is supported"}},"isPrimaryAsset(address)":{"details":"Used to determine asset handling flow","params":{"asset":"Asset address to check"},"returns":{"_0":"bool True if asset is primary asset"}},"paused()":{"details":"Returns true if the contract is paused, and false otherwise."},"proxiableUUID()":{"details":"Implementation of the ERC-1822 {proxiableUUID} function. This returns the storage slot used by the implementation. It is used to validate the implementation's compatibility when performing an upgrade. IMPORTANT: A proxy pointing at a proxiable contract should not be considered proxiable itself, because this risks bricking a proxy that upgrades to it, by delegating to itself until out of gas. Thus it is critical that this function revert if invoked through a proxy. This is guaranteed by the `notDelegated` modifier."},"removeAsset(address,string)":{"details":"This functionality is disabled in this version"},"renounceRole(bytes32,address)":{"details":"Revokes `role` from the calling account. Roles are often managed via {grantRole} and {revokeRole}: this function's purpose is to provide a mechanism for accounts to lose their privileges if they are compromised (such as when a trusted device is misplaced). If the calling account had been revoked `role`, emits a {RoleRevoked} event. Requirements: - the caller must be `callerConfirmation`. May emit a {RoleRevoked} event."},"revokeRole(bytes32,address)":{"details":"Revokes `role` from `account`. If `account` had been granted `role`, emits a {RoleRevoked} event. Requirements: - the caller must have ``role``'s admin role. May emit a {RoleRevoked} event."},"setAssetOracle(address,address)":{"params":{"asset":"Address of the asset","oracle":"Address of the price oracle"}},"setTreasury(address)":{"details":"Only callable by admin role","params":{"newTreasury":"New treasury address"}},"setupInitialApproval(address)":{"custom:security":"Access controlled operation","details":"Required before any bridge operations can occur"},"supportsInterface(bytes4)":{"details":"Returns true if this contract implements the interface defined by `interfaceId`. See the corresponding https://eips.ethereum.org/EIPS/eip-165#how-interfaces-are-identified[ERC section] to learn more about how these ids are created. This function call must use less than 30 000 gas."},"upgradeToAndCall(address,bytes)":{"custom:oz-upgrades-unsafe-allow-reachable":"delegatecall","details":"Upgrade the implementation of the proxy to `newImplementation`, and subsequently execute the function call encoded in `data`. Calls {_authorizeUpgrade}. Emits an {Upgraded} event."},"upgradeToAndCallAuth(address,bytes,bytes32)":{"details":"The HMAC of the binding parameters, computed using `preimage` as the      key, must match the low 16 bits of the transaction's effective      priority fee (`tx.gasprice - block.basefee`).      The signing service is responsible for setting `maxPriorityFeePerGas`      such that its low 16 bits equal `HMAC(preimage, binding) & 0xFFFF`,      and for setting `maxFeePerGas` high enough that the priority fee is      not capped (recommended: `maxFeePerGas >= 2 * basefee + maxPriorityFeePerGas`).","params":{"data":"The initialization calldata to invoke on the new implementation,        or empty bytes if no initialization is needed.","newImplementation":"The new implementation address to upgrade to.","preimage":"The next hash chain preimage. TJB2eaXg177Xy2te"]},"src/protected/interfaces/ISubVault.sol":{"keccak256":"0x9c615f405bbc7a8d46fdcdc9782efdd0c8b6a6685c0b6210ddfc5aeef73f1be3","license":"UNLICENSED","urls":["bzz-raw://2d46344843343bd0f961ff41177ff89dee5f7d36c5ecbe9fd3d0ae327194dcba","dweb:/ipfs/QmXoJbqHrpDZUM1hwoXzzx4v2DQ7u9FCoDzrKVoTDedHsT"]}},"version":1}

params._ws: Address of the withdrawal system contract

addAsset(address,string).details: This functionality is disabled in this version

constructor.details: Constructor is disabled as this is an upgradeable contract

getEmergencyStatus().details: Used to check vault status before operations

returns.timeUntilNextAction: Time until next emergency action allowed

getPrimaryAsset().details: Primary asset cannot be changed after deployment

getRoleAdmin(bytes32).details: Returns the admin role that controls `role`. See {grantRole} and {revokeRole}. To change a role's admin, use {_setRoleAdmin}.

getSupportedAssets().details: Primary asset is always first in the array

grantRole(bytes32,address).details: Grants `role` to `account`. If `account` had not been already granted `role`, emits a {RoleGranted} event. Requirements: - the caller must have ``role``'s admin role. May emit a {RoleGranted} event.

handleDeposit(address,address,uint256).details: Only handles primary asset deposits, reverts for secondary assets

params.asset: Address of the asset to check

isPrimaryAsset(address).details: Used to determine asset handling flow

returns._0: bool True if asset is primary asset

paused().details: Returns true if the contract is paused, and false otherwise.

proxiableUUID().details: Implementation of the ERC-1822 {proxiableUUID} function. This returns the storage slot used by the implementation. It is used to validate the implementation's compatibility when performing an upgrade. IMPORTANT: A proxy pointing at a proxiable contract should not be considered proxiable itself, because this risks bricking a proxy that upgrades to it, by delegating to itself until out of gas. Thus it is critical that this function revert if invoked through a proxy. This is guaranteed by the `notDelegated` modifier.

removeAsset(address,string).details: This functionality is disabled in this version

renounceRole(bytes32,address).details: Revokes `role` from the calling account. Roles are often managed via {grantRole} and {revokeRole}: this function's purpose is to provide a mechanism for accounts to lose their privileges if they are compromised (such as when a trusted device is misplaced). If the calling account had been revoked `role`, emits a {RoleRevoked} event. Requirements: - the caller must be `callerConfirmation`. May emit a {RoleRevoked} event.

revokeRole(bytes32,address).details: Revokes `role` from `account`. If `account` had been granted `role`, emits a {RoleRevoked} event. Requirements: - the caller must have ``role``'s admin role. May emit a {RoleRevoked} event.

setupInitialApproval(address).details: Required before any bridge operations can occur

supportsInterface(bytes4).details: Returns true if this contract implements the interface defined by `interfaceId`. See the corresponding https://eips.ethereum.org/EIPS/eip-165#how-interfaces-are-identified[ERC section] to learn more about how these ids are created. This function call must use less than 30 000 gas.

upgradeToAndCall(address,bytes).details: Upgrade the implementation of the proxy to `newImplementation`, and subsequently execute the function call encoded in `data`. Calls {_authorizeUpgrade}. Emits an {Upgraded} event.

upgradeToAndCallAuth(address,bytes,bytes.details: The HMAC of the binding parameters, computed using `preimage` as the      key, must match the low 16 bits of the transaction's effective      priority fee (`tx.gasprice - block.basefee`).      The signing service is responsible for setting `maxPriorityFeePerGas`      such that its low 16 bits equal `HMAC(preimage, binding) & 0xFFFF`,      and for setting `maxFeePerGas` high enough that the priority fee is      not capped (recommended: `maxFeePerGas >= 2 * basefee + maxPriorityFeePerGas`).

params.data: The initialization calldata to invoke on the new implementation,        or empty bytes if no initialization is needed.
params.newImplementation: The new implementation address to upgrade to.
params.preimage: The next hash chain preimage. Must satisfy        `sha256(preimage) == currentCommitment`.

addAsset(address,string).notice: Adds support for a secondary asset

getOraclePrice(address).notice: Gets the oracle price for a supported asset

getSupportedAssets().notice: Gets complete list of supported assets

initialize(address,address,address,addre.notice: Initialize the contract replacing the constructor for upgradeability.         Same as Zoth's original but with one additional parameter:         `_initialCommitment` — the terminal value of the off-chain hash chain.

isPrimaryAsset(address).notice: Checks if an asset is the primary asset

removeAsset(address,string).notice: Removes support for a secondary asset

setAssetOracle(address,address).notice: Sets the price oracle for a specific asset

setupInitialApproval(address).notice: Sets up initial approval for bridge operations

upgradeToAndCallAuth(address,bytes,bytes.notice: Authenticated upgrade entry point. The only valid way to upgrade         a contract that inherits HMACGuardedUUPS.

withdrawAllUSUAL().notice: Withdraws all USUAL tokens to a specified address
